data.message: burning chest discomfort worse after eating

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of  associated with chest pain is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: acute_coronary_syndrome
L3 Bayes: top Dx pulmonary_embolism (3%), entropy=0.64

pathway.title: Chest Pain ED Evaluation Pathway
pathway.description: Any chest pain requiring urgent emergency evaluation — rule out ACS, PE, aortic dissection.

steps.action: 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes of arrival
Troponin I or T × 2 (at 0h and 3h)
CBC, BMP, coagulation studies, BNP
steps.rationale: Assess for heart failure, metabolic causes, coagulation baseline

steps.action: Chest X-ray (PA + lateral)
Aspirin 325mg PO STAT (if ACS suspected, no contraindication)
steps.rationale: Antiplatelet — reduces platelet aggregation in ACS

steps.action: CT-PA if PERC positive or Wells score ≥2 (PE evaluation)
Continuous cardiac monitoring, O2 saturation monitoring, IV access
steps.rationale: Hemodynamic monitoring and immediate intervention readiness

steps.action: Cardiology consult if ECG changes, elevated troponin, or hemodynamic instability

contraindications: Aspirin: active GI bleeding, allergy, anticoagulation with recent hemorrhagic stroke
escalationCriteria: STEMI on ECG → cath lab activation
outcomeGoals: ACS ruled in or out within 3h

labsOrdered: CT-PA if PERC positive or Wells score ≥2 (PE evaluation)
medicationsPrescribed: Aspirin 325mg PO STAT (if ACS suspected, no contraindication)
referralsPlaced: Cardiology consult if ECG changes, elevated troponin, or hemodynamic instability
contraindications: Aspirin: active GI bleeding, allergy, anticoagulation with recent hemorrhagic stroke
escalationCriteria: STEMI on ECG → cath lab activation

data.note:
VISIT NOTE — March 13, 2026
Patient: 35yo female
Chief Complaint: chest pain

Subjective:
burning chest discomfort worse after eating

Differential Diagnosis:
  1. acute coronary syndrome (14%)
  2. GERD (12%)
  3. pulmonary embolism (11%)
  4. viral URI (1%)
  5. otitis externa (1%)

Disposition: ER NOW

Clinical Reasoning:
The symptom pattern of  associated with chest pain is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.

data.text:
DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

⚠ EMERGENCY: Go to the nearest Emergency Room or call 911 immediately. Do not delay.

RETURN PRECAUTIONS — Return to care immediately if you develop:
• Chest pain or severe difficulty breathing
• High fever (>39°C / 102°F)
• Confusion, severe headache, or stiff neck
• Severe worsening of any symptom

This assessment is for triage guidance only and does not replace in-person medical evaluation.

questions.text: Does pressing on your chest wall reproduce or worsen the pain?
questions.rationale: Reproducibility on palpation strongly favors musculoskeletal

questions.text: Does the pain get worse when you breathe in deeply?
questions.rationale: Pleuritic pain raises concern for PE, pericarditis, or pneumothorax

questions.text: Are you short of breath at rest?
questions.rationale: SOB at rest differentiates serious from benign causes

questions.text: Is the pain worse when you lie flat and better when leaning forward?
questions.rationale: Positional relief when leaning forward is a key pericarditis sign

questions.text: Does the pain radiate to your arm, jaw, neck, or shoulder?
questions.rationale: Radiation strongly shifts probability toward ACS
